description: Two folios of this 12-folio manuscript of the Book of the Calendar Controversy, about the disagreement between Jewish leaders of Palestine and Babylonia on how to calculate the calendar year in 921/2. This led the Jews of the entire Near East to celebrate Passover and the other festivals, through two years, on different dates. The controversy was major, but it became forgotten until its late 19th-century rediscovery in the Cairo Genizah. The two folios found are T-S NS 194.92, the second, and T-S 8K4, the fifth. Stern supposes that the manuscript is twelve parts and that extant here are the second and fifth parts because of comparison to parallel manuscripts.
description_he: שני דפים מכתב יד בן 12 דפים של 'ספר המחלוקת על הלוח', העוסק בוויכוח בין מנהיגי יהודי ארץ ישראל ובבל על חישוב השנה בשנת 921/22. הוויכוח גרם ליהודי המזרח התיכון לחגוג את פסח ואת שאר החגים בתאריכים שונים. הדפים הם ת-ס NS 194.92 (השני) ות-ס 8K4 (החמישי).
